params.name: Yankee Doodle
params.description:
On Jul 30, 1777 AD, a group of British soldiers surrendered to the American army with the melody of Yankee Doodle.
Yankee Doodle, also called Yankee Song, is a well-known American folk tune. It's a mix of insults hurled by the British at the Americans, including "Yankee" as an insult for rural folks. But Americans liked the tune so much that they used it as a military song, and it became part of their upbeat and uncomplicated cultural heritage.
